111	
112	    @classmethod
113	    def register_for_auto_class(cls, auto_class="AutoModel"):
114	        """
115	        Register this class with the given auto class so that it can be loaded with `AutoModel.from_pretrained(...,
116	        trust_remote_code=True)`.
117	
118	        When the config is saved, the resulting `config.json` will include an `auto_map` entry mapping the auto class
119	        to this class's module and class name.
120	
121	        Args:
122	            auto_class (`str` or type, *optional*, defaults to `"AutoModel"`):
123	                The auto class to register this class with. Can be a string (e.g. `"AutoModel"`) or the class itself.
124	                Currently only `"AutoModel"` is supported.
125	
126	        Example:
127	
128	        ```python
129	        from diffusers import ModelMixin, ConfigMixin
130	
131	
132	        class MyCustomModel(ModelMixin, ConfigMixin): ...
133	
134	
135	        MyCustomModel.register_for_auto_class("AutoModel")
136	        ```
137	        """
138	        if auto_class != "AutoModel":
139	            raise ValueError(f"Only 'AutoModel' is supported, got '{auto_class}'.")
140	
141	        cls._auto_class = auto_class
142	
143	    def register_to_config(self, **kwargs):
144	        if self.config_name is None:
145	            raise NotImplementedError(f"Make sure that {self.__class__} has defined a class name `config_name`")
146	        # Special case for `kwargs` used in deprecation warning added to schedulers
147	        # TODO: remove this when we remove the deprecation warning, and the `kwargs` argument,
148	        # or solve in a more general way.
149	        kwargs.pop("kwargs", None)
150	
151	        if not hasattr(self, "_internal_dict"):
152	            internal_dict = kwargs
153	        else:
154	            previous_dict = dict(self._internal_dict)
155	            internal_dict = {**self._internal_dict, **kwargs}
156	            logger.debug(f"Updating config from {previous_dict} to {internal_dict}")
157	
158	        self._internal_dict = FrozenDict(internal_dict)
159	
160	    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
161	        """The only reason we overwrite `getattr` here is to gracefully deprecate accessing
162	        config attributes directly. See https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers/pull/3129
163	
164	        This function is mostly copied from PyTorch's __getattr__ overwrite:
165	        https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/_modules/torch/nn/modules/module.html#Module
166	        """
167	
168	        is_in_config = "_internal_dict" in self.__dict__ and hasattr(self.__dict__["_internal_dict"], name)
169	        is_attribute = name in self.__dict__
170	
171	        if is_in_config and not is_attribute:
172	            deprecation_message = f"Accessing config attribute `{name}` directly via '{type(self).__name__}' object attribute is deprecated. Please access '{name}' over '{type(self).__name__}'s config object instead, e.g. 'scheduler.config.{name}'."
173	            deprecate("direct config name access", "1.0.0", deprecation_message, standard_warn=False)
174	            return self._internal_dict[name]
175	
176	        raise AttributeError(f"'{type(self).__name__}' object has no attribute '{name}'")
177	
178	    def save_config(self, save_directory: str | os.PathLike, push_to_hub: bool = False, **kwargs):
179	        """
180	        Save a configuration object to the directory specified in `save_directory` so that it can be reloaded using the
181	        [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] class method.
182	
183	        Args:
184	            save_directory (`str` or `os.PathLike`):
185	                Directory where the configuration JSON file is saved (will be created if it does not exist).
186	            push_to_hub (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
187	                Whether or not to push your model to the Hugging Face Hub after saving it. You can specify the
188	                repository you want to push to with `repo_id` (will default to the name of `save_directory` in your
189	                namespace).
190	            kwargs (`dict[str, Any]`, *optional*):
191	                Additional keyword arguments passed along to the [`~utils.PushToHubMixin.push_to_hub`] method.
192	        """
193	        if os.path.isfile(save_directory):
194	            raise AssertionError(f"Provided path ({save_directory}) should be a directory, not a file")
195	
196	        os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)
197	
198	        # If we save using the predefined names, we can load using `from_config`
199	        output_config_file = os.path.join(save_directory, self.config_name)
200	
201	        self.to_json_file(output_config_file)
202	        logger.info(f"Configuration saved in {output_config_file}")
203	
204	        if push_to_hub:
205	            commit_message = kwargs.pop("commit_message", None)
206	            private = kwargs.pop("private", None)
207	            create_pr = kwargs.pop("create_pr", False)
208	            token = kwargs.pop("token", None)
209	            repo_id = kwargs.pop("repo_id", save_directory.split(os.path.sep)[-1])
210	            repo_id = create_repo(repo_id, exist_ok=True, private=private, token=token).repo_id
211	            subfolder = kwargs.pop("subfolder", None)
212	
213	            self._upload_folder(
214	                save_directory,
215	                repo_id,
216	                token=token,
217	                commit_message=commit_message,
218	                create_pr=create_pr,
219	                subfolder=subfolder,
220	            )
221	
222	    @classmethod
223	    def from_config(
224	        cls, config: FrozenDict | dict[str, Any] = None, return_unused_kwargs=False, **kwargs
225	    ) -> Self | tuple[Self, dict[str, Any]]:
226	        r"""
227	        Instantiate a Python class from a config dictionary.
228	
229	        Parameters:
230	            config (`dict[str, Any]`):
231	                A config dictionary from which the Python class is instantiated. Make sure to only load configuration
232	                files of compatible classes.
233	            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
234	                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
235	            kwargs (remaining dictionary of keyword arguments, *optional*):
236	                Can be used to update the configuration object (after it is loaded) and initiate the Python class.
237	                `**kwargs` are passed directly to the underlying scheduler/model's `__init__` method and eventually
238	                overwrite the same named arguments in `config`.
239	
240	        Returns:
241	            [`ModelMixin`] or [`SchedulerMixin`]:
242	                A model or scheduler object instantiated from a config dictionary.
243	
244	        Examples:
245	
246	        ```python
247	        >>> from diffusers import DDPMScheduler, DDIMScheduler, PNDMScheduler
248	
249	        >>> # Download scheduler from huggingface.co and cache.
250	        >>> scheduler = DDPMScheduler.from_pretrained("google/ddpm-cifar10-32")
251	
252	        >>> # Instantiate DDIM scheduler class with same config as DDPM
253	        >>> scheduler = DDIMScheduler.from_config(scheduler.config)
254	
255	        >>> # Instantiate PNDM scheduler class with same config as DDPM
256	        >>> scheduler = PNDMScheduler.from_config(scheduler.config)
257	        ```
258	        """
259	        # <===== TO BE REMOVED WITH DEPRECATION
260	        # TODO(Patrick) - make sure to remove the following lines when config=="model_path" is deprecated
261	        if "pretrained_model_name_or_path" in kwargs:
262	            config = kwargs.pop("pretrained_model_name_or_path")
263	
264	        if config is None:
265	            raise ValueError("Please make sure to provide a config as the first positional argument.")
266	        # ======>
267	
268	        if not isinstance(config, dict):
269	            deprecation_message = "It is deprecated to pass a pretrained model name or path to `from_config`."
270	            if "Scheduler" in cls.__name__:
271	                deprecation_message += (
272	                    f"If you were trying to load a scheduler, please use {cls}.from_pretrained(...) instead."
273	                    " Otherwise, please make sure to pass a configuration dictionary instead. This functionality will"
274	                    " be removed in v1.0.0."
275	                )
276	            elif "Model" in cls.__name__:
277	                deprecation_message += (
278	                    f"If you were trying to load a model, please use {cls}.load_config(...) followed by"
279	                    f" {cls}.from_config(...) instead. Otherwise, please make sure to pass a configuration dictionary"
280	                    " instead. This functionality will be removed in v1.0.0."
281	                )
282	            deprecate("config-passed-as-path", "1.0.0", deprecation_message, standard_warn=False)
283	            config, kwargs = cls.load_config(pretrained_model_name_or_path=config, return_unused_kwargs=True, **kwargs)
284	
285	        init_dict, unused_kwargs, hidden_dict = cls.extract_init_dict(config, **kwargs)
286	
287	        # Allow dtype to be specified on initialization
288	        if "dtype" in unused_kwargs:
289	            init_dict["dtype"] = unused_kwargs.pop("dtype")
290	
291	        # add possible deprecated kwargs
292	        for deprecated_kwarg in cls._deprecated_kwargs:
293	            if deprecated_kwarg in unused_kwargs:
294	                init_dict[deprecated_kwarg] = unused_kwargs.pop(deprecated_kwarg)
295	
296	        # Return model and optionally state and/or unused_kwargs
297	        model = cls(**init_dict)
298	
299	        # make sure to also save config parameters that might be used for compatible classes
300	        # update _class_name
301	        if "_class_name" in hidden_dict:
302	            hidden_dict["_class_name"] = cls.__name__
303	
304	        model.register_to_config(**hidden_dict)
305	
306	        # add hidden kwargs of compatible classes to unused_kwargs
307	        unused_kwargs = {**unused_kwargs, **hidden_dict}
308	
309	        if return_unused_kwargs:
310	            return (model, unused_kwargs)
311	        else:
312	            return model
313	
314	    @classmethod
315	    def get_config_dict(cls, *args, **kwargs):
316	        deprecation_message = (
317	            f" The function get_config_dict is deprecated. Please use {cls}.load_config instead. This function will be"
318	            " removed in version v1.0.0"
319	        )
320	        deprecate("get_config_dict", "1.0.0", deprecation_message, standard_warn=False)
321	        return cls.load_config(*args, **kwargs)
322	
323	    @classmethod
324	    @validate_hf_hub_args
325	    def load_config(
326	        cls,
327	        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike,
328	        return_unused_kwargs=False,
329	        return_commit_hash=False,
330	        **kwargs,
331	    ) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]]:
332	        r"""
333	        Load a model or scheduler configuration.
334	
335	        Parameters:
336	            pretrained_model_name_or_path (`str` or `os.PathLike`, *optional*):
337	                Can be either:
338	
339	                    - A string, the *model id* (for example `google/ddpm-celebahq-256`) of a pretrained model hosted on
340	                      the Hub.
341	                    - A path to a *directory* (for example `./my_model_directory`) containing model weights saved with
342	                      [`~ConfigMixin.save_config`].
343	
344	            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):
345	                Path to a directory where a downloaded pretrained model configuration is cached if the standard cache
346	                is not used.
347	            force_download (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
348	                Whether or not to force the (re-)download of the model weights and configuration files, overriding the
349	                cached versions if they exist.
350	            proxies (`dict[str, str]`, *optional*):
351	                A dictionary of proxy servers to use by protocol or endpoint, for example, `{'http': 'foo.bar:3128',
352	                'http://hostname': 'foo.bar:4012'}`. The proxies are used on each request.
353	            output_loading_info(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
354	                Whether or not to also return a dictionary containing missing keys, unexpected keys and error messages.
355	            local_files_only (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
356	                Whether to only load local model weights and configuration files or not. If set to `True`, the model
357	                won't be downloaded from the Hub.
358	            token (`str` or *bool*, *optional*):
359	                The token to use as HTTP bearer authorization for remote files. If `True`, the token generated from
360	                `diffusers-cli login` (stored in `~/.huggingface`) is used.
361	            revision (`str`, *optional*, defaults to `"main"`):
362	                The specific model version to use. It can be a branch name, a tag name, a commit id, or any identifier
363	                allowed by Git.
364	            subfolder (`str`, *optional*, defaults to `""`):
365	                The subfolder location of a model file within a larger model repository on the Hub or locally.
366	            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False):
367	                Whether unused keyword arguments of the config are returned.
368	            return_commit_hash (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False):
369	                Whether the `commit_hash` of the loaded configuration are returned.
370	
371	        Returns:
372	            `dict`:
373	                A dictionary of all the parameters stored in a JSON configuration file.
374	
375	        """
376	        cache_dir = kwargs.pop("cache_dir", None)
377	        local_dir = kwargs.pop("local_dir", None)
378	        local_dir_use_symlinks = kwargs.pop("local_dir_use_symlinks", "auto")
379	        force_download = kwargs.pop("force_download", False)
380	        proxies = kwargs.pop("proxies", None)
381	        token = kwargs.pop("token", None)
382	        local_files_only = kwargs.pop("local_files_only", False)
383	        revision = kwargs.pop("revision", None)
384	        _ = kwargs.pop("mirror", None)
385	        subfolder = kwargs.pop("subfolder", None)
386	        user_agent = kwargs.pop("user_agent", {})
387	        dduf_entries: dict[str, DDUFEntry] | None = kwargs.pop("dduf_entries", None)
388	
389	        user_agent = {**user_agent, "file_type": "config"}
390	        user_agent = http_user_agent(user_agent)
391	
392	        pretrained_model_name_or_path = str(pretrained_model_name_or_path)
393	
394	        if cls.config_name is None:
395	            raise ValueError(
396	                "`self.config_name` is not defined. Note that one should not load a config from "
397	                "`ConfigMixin`. Please make sure to define `config_name` in a class inheriting from `ConfigMixin`"
398	            )
399	        # Custom path for now
400	        if dduf_entries:
401	            if subfolder is not None:
402	                raise ValueError(
403	                    "DDUF file only allow for 1 level of directory (e.g transformer/model1/model.safetentors is not allowed). "
404	                    "Please check the DDUF structure"
405	                )
406	            config_file = cls._get_config_file_from_dduf(pretrained_model_name_or_path, dduf_entries)
407	        elif os.path.isfile(pretrained_model_name_or_path):
408	            config_file = pretrained_model_name_or_path
409	        elif os.path.isdir(pretrained_model_name_or_path):
410	            if subfolder is not None and os.path.isfile(
411	                os.path.join(pretrained_model_name_or_path, subfolder, cls.config_name)
412	            ):
413	                config_file = os.path.join(pretrained_model_name_or_path, subfolder, cls.config_name)
414	            elif os.path.isfile(os.path.join(pretrained_model_name_or_path, cls.config_name)):
415	                # Load from a PyTorch checkpoint
416	                config_file = os.path.join(pretrained_model_name_or_path, cls.config_name)
417	            else:
418	                raise EnvironmentError(
419	                    f"Error no file named {cls.config_name} found in directory {pretrained_model_name_or_path}."
420	                )
421	        else:
422	            try:
423	                # Load from URL or cache if already cached
424	                config_file = hf_hub_download(
425	                    pretrained_model_name_or_path,
426	                    filename=cls.config_name,
427	                    cache_dir=cache_dir,
428	                    force_download=force_download,
429	                    proxies=proxies,
430	                    local_files_only=local_files_only,
431	                    token=token,
432	                    user_agent=user_agent,
433	                    subfolder=subfolder,
434	                    revision=revision,
435	                    local_dir=local_dir,
436	                    local_dir_use_symlinks=local_dir_use_symlinks,
437	                )
438	            except RepositoryNotFoundError:
439	                raise EnvironmentError(
440	                    f"{pretrained_model_name_or_path} is not a local folder and is not a valid model identifier"
441	                    " listed on 'https://huggingface.co/models'\nIf this is a private repository, make sure to pass a"
442	                    " token having permission to this repo with `token` or log in with `hf auth login`."
443	                )
444	            except RevisionNotFoundError:
445	                raise EnvironmentError(
446	                    f"{revision} is not a valid git identifier (branch name, tag name or commit id) that exists for"
447	                    " this model name. Check the model page at"
448	                    f" 'https://huggingface.co/{pretrained_model_name_or_path}' for available revisions."
449	                )
450	            except EntryNotFoundError:
451	                raise EnvironmentError(
452	                    f"{pretrained_model_name_or_path} does not appear to have a file named {cls.config_name}."
453	                )
454	            except HfHubHTTPError as err:
455	                raise EnvironmentError(
456	                    "There was a specific connection error when trying to load"
457	                    f" {pretrained_model_name_or_path}:\n{err}"
458	                )
459	            except ValueError:
460	                raise EnvironmentError(
461	                    f"We couldn't connect to '{HUGGINGFACE_CO_RESOLVE_ENDPOINT}' to load this model, couldn't find it"
462	                    f" in the cached files and it looks like {pretrained_model_name_or_path} is not the path to a"
463	                    f" directory containing a {cls.config_name} file.\nCheckout your internet connection or see how to"
464	                    " run the library in offline mode at"
465	                    " 'https://huggingface.co/docs/diffusers/installation#offline-mode'."
466	                )
467	            except EnvironmentError:
468	                raise EnvironmentError(
469	                    f"Can't load config for '{pretrained_model_name_or_path}'. If you were trying to load it from "
470	                    "'https://huggingface.co/models', make sure you don't have a local directory with the same name. "
471	                    f"Otherwise, make sure '{pretrained_model_name_or_path}' is the correct path to a directory "
472	                    f"containing a {cls.config_name} file"
473	                )
474	        try:
475	            config_dict = cls._dict_from_json_file(config_file, dduf_entries=dduf_entries)
476	
477	            commit_hash = extract_commit_hash(config_file)
478	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError):
479	            raise EnvironmentError(f"It looks like the config file at '{config_file}' is not a valid JSON file.")
480	
481	        if not (return_unused_kwargs or return_commit_hash):
482	            return config_dict
483	
484	        outputs = (config_dict,)
485	
486	        if return_unused_kwargs:
487	            outputs += (kwargs,)
488	
489	        if return_commit_hash:
490	            outputs += (commit_hash,)
491	
492	        return outputs
493	
494	    @staticmethod
495	    def _get_init_keys(input_class):
496	        return set(dict(inspect.signature(input_class.__init__).parameters).keys())
497	
498	    @classmethod
499	    def extract_init_dict(cls, config_dict, **kwargs):
500	        # Skip keys that were not present in the original config, so default __init__ values were used
501	        used_defaults = config_dict.get("_use_default_values", [])
502	        config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k not in used_defaults and k != "_use_default_values"}
503	
504	        # 0. Copy origin config dict
505	        original_dict = dict(config_dict.items())
506	
507	        # 1. Retrieve expected config attributes from __init__ signature
508	        expected_keys = cls._get_init_keys(cls)
509	        expected_keys.remove("self")
510	        # remove general kwargs if present in dict
511	        if "kwargs" in expected_keys:
512	            expected_keys.remove("kwargs")
513	        # remove flax internal keys
514	        if hasattr(cls, "_flax_internal_args"):
515	            for arg in cls._flax_internal_args:
516	                expected_keys.remove(arg)
517	
518	        # 2. Remove attributes that cannot be expected from expected config attributes
519	        # remove keys to be ignored
520	        if len(cls.ignore_for_config) > 0:
521	            expected_keys = expected_keys - set(cls.ignore_for_config)
522	
523	        # load diffusers library to import compatible and original scheduler
524	        diffusers_library = importlib.import_module(__name__.split(".")[0])
525	
526	        if cls.has_compatibles:
527	            compatible_classes = [c for c in cls._get_compatibles() if not isinstance(c, DummyObject)]
528	        else:
529	            compatible_classes = []
530	
531	        expected_keys_comp_cls = set()
532	        for c in compatible_classes:
533	            expected_keys_c = cls._get_init_keys(c)
534	            expected_keys_comp_cls = expected_keys_comp_cls.union(expected_keys_c)
535	        expected_keys_comp_cls = expected_keys_comp_cls - cls._get_init_keys(cls)
536	        config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k not in expected_keys_comp_cls}
537	
538	        # remove attributes from orig class that cannot be expected
539	        orig_cls_name = config_dict.pop("_class_name", cls.__name__)
540	        if (
541	            isinstance(orig_cls_name, str)
542	            and orig_cls_name != cls.__name__
543	            and hasattr(diffusers_library, orig_cls_name)
544	        ):
545	            orig_cls = getattr(diffusers_library, orig_cls_name)
546	            unexpected_keys_from_orig = cls._get_init_keys(orig_cls) - expected_keys
547	            config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k not in unexpected_keys_from_orig}
548	        elif not isinstance(orig_cls_name, str) and not isinstance(orig_cls_name, (list, tuple)):
549	            raise ValueError(
550	                "Make sure that the `_class_name` is of type string or list of string (for custom pipelines)."
551	            )
552	
553	        # remove private attributes
554	        config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
555	
556	        # remove quantization_config
557	        config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k != "quantization_config"}
558	
559	        # 3. Create keyword arguments that will be passed to __init__ from expected keyword arguments
560	        init_dict = {}
561	        for key in expected_keys:
562	            # if config param is passed to kwarg and is present in config dict
563	            # it should overwrite existing config dict key
564	            if key in kwargs and key in config_dict:
565	                config_dict[key] = kwargs.pop(key)
566	
567	            if key in kwargs:
568	                # overwrite key
569	                init_dict[key] = kwargs.pop(key)
570	            elif key in config_dict:
571	                # use value from config dict
572	                init_dict[key] = config_dict.pop(key)
573	
574	        # 4. Give nice warning if unexpected values have been passed
575	        if len(config_dict) > 0:
576	            logger.warning(
577	                f"The config attributes {config_dict} were passed to {cls.__name__}, "
578	                "but are not expected and will be ignored. Please verify your "
579	                f"{cls.config_name} configuration file."
580	            )
581	
582	        # 5. Give nice info if config attributes are initialized to default because they have not been passed
583	        passed_keys = set(init_dict.keys())
584	        if len(expected_keys - passed_keys) > 0:
585	            logger.info(
586	                f"{expected_keys - passed_keys} was not found in config. Values will be initialized to default values."
587	            )
588	
589	        # 6. Define unused keyword arguments
590	        unused_kwargs = {**config_dict, **kwargs}
591	
592	        # 7. Define "hidden" config parameters that were saved for compatible classes
593	        hidden_config_dict = {k: v for k, v in original_dict.items() if k not in init_dict}
594	
595	        return init_dict, unused_kwargs, hidden_config_dict
596	
597	    @classmethod
598	    def _dict_from_json_file(cls, json_file: str | os.PathLike, dduf_entries: dict[str, DDUFEntry] | None = None):
599	        if dduf_entries:
600	            text = dduf_entries[json_file].read_text()
601	        else:
602	            with open(json_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as reader:
603	                text = reader.read()
604	        return json.loads(text)
605	
606	    def __repr__(self):
607	        return f"{self.__class__.__name__} {self.to_json_string()}"
608	
609	    @property
610	    def config(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
611	        """
612	        Returns the config of the class as a frozen dictionary
613	
614	        Returns:
615	            `dict[str, Any]`: Config of the class.
616	        """
617	        return self._internal_dict
618	
619	    def to_json_string(self) -> str:
620	        """
621	        Serializes the configuration instance to a JSON string.
622	
623	        Returns:
624	            `str`:
625	                String containing all the attributes that make up the configuration instance in JSON format.
626	        """
627	        config_dict = self._internal_dict if hasattr(self, "_internal_dict") else {}

690	def register_to_config(init):
691	    r"""
692	    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from [`ConfigMixin`] so that all the arguments are
693	    automatically sent to `self.register_for_config`. To ignore a specific argument accepted by the init but that
694	    shouldn't be registered in the config, use the `ignore_for_config` class variable
695	
696	    Warning: Once decorated, all private arguments (beginning with an underscore) are trashed and not sent to the init!
697	    """
698	
699	    @functools.wraps(init)
700	    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
701	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init.
702	        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
703	        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
704	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
705	            raise RuntimeError(
706	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
707	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
708	            )
709	
710	        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
711	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
712	        new_kwargs = {}
713	        signature = inspect.signature(init)
714	        parameters = {
715	            name: p.default for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) if i > 0 and name not in ignore
716	        }
717	        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
718	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
719	
720	        # Then add all kwargs
721	        new_kwargs.update(
722	            {
723	                k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
724	                for k, default in parameters.items()
725	                if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
726	            }
727	        )
728	
729	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
730	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
731	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
732	
733	        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
734	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
735	        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)
736	
737	    return inner_init

752	        init_kwargs = dict(kwargs.items())

788	class LegacyConfigMixin(ConfigMixin):
789	    r"""
790	    A subclass of `ConfigMixin` to resolve class mapping from legacy classes (like `Transformer2DModel`) to more
791	    pipeline-specific classes (like `DiTTransformer2DModel`).

794	    @classmethod

801	
802	        if remapped_class is cls:
803	            return super(LegacyConfigMixin, remapped_class).from_config(config, return_unused_kwargs, **kwargs)

51	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddpm.betas_for_alpha_bar
52	def betas_for_alpha_bar(
53	    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
54	    max_beta: float = 0.999,
55	    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
56	) -> torch.Tensor:
57	    """
58	    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function, which defines the cumulative product of
59	    (1-beta) over time from t = [0,1].
60	
61	    Contains a function alpha_bar that takes an argument t and transforms it to the cumulative product of (1-beta) up
62	    to that part of the diffusion process.
63	
64	    Args:
65	        num_diffusion_timesteps (`int`):
66	            The number of betas to produce.
67	        max_beta (`float`, defaults to `0.999`):
68	            The maximum beta to use; use values lower than 1 to avoid numerical instability.
69	        alpha_transform_type (`str`, defaults to `"cosine"`):
70	            The type of noise schedule for `alpha_bar`. Choose from `cosine`, `exp`, or `laplace`.
71	
72	    Returns:
73	        `torch.Tensor`:
74	            The betas used by the scheduler to step the model outputs.
75	    """
76	    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
77	
78	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
79	            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2
80	
81	    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
82	
83	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
84	            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
85	            snr = math.exp(lmb)
86	            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
87	
88	    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
89	
90	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
91	            return math.exp(t * -12.0)
92	
93	    else:
94	        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported alpha_transform_type: {alpha_transform_type}")
95	
96	    betas = []
97	    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
98	        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
99	        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
100	        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
101	    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)
102

104	class LMSDiscreteScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
105	    """
106	    A linear multistep scheduler for discrete beta schedules.
107	
108	    This model inherits from [`SchedulerMixin`] and [`ConfigMixin`]. Check the superclass documentation for the generic
109	    methods the library implements for all schedulers such as loading and saving.
110	
111	    Args:
112	        num_train_timesteps (`int`, defaults to `1000`):
113	            The number of diffusion steps to train the model.
114	        beta_start (`float`, defaults to `0.0001`):
115	            The starting `beta` value of inference.
116	        beta_end (`float`, defaults to `0.02`):
117	            The final `beta` value.
118	        beta_schedule (`"linear"`, `"scaled_linear"`, or `"squaredcos_cap_v2"`, defaults to `"linear"`):
119	            The beta schedule, a mapping from a beta range to a sequence of betas for stepping the model.
120	        trained_betas (`np.ndarray`, *optional*):
121	            Pass an array of betas directly to the constructor to bypass `beta_start` and `beta_end`.
122	        use_karras_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
123	            Whether to use Karras sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process. If `True`,
124	            the sigmas are determined according to a sequence of noise levels {σi}.
125	        use_exponential_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
126	            Whether to use exponential sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process.
127	        use_beta_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
128	            Whether to use beta sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process. Refer to [Beta
129	            Sampling is All You Need](https://huggingface.co/papers/2407.12173) for more information.
130	        prediction_type (`"epsilon"`, `"sample"`, or `"v_prediction"`, defaults to `"epsilon"`):
131	            Prediction type of the scheduler function; can be `epsilon` (predicts the noise of the diffusion process),
132	            `sample` (directly predicts the noisy sample`) or `v_prediction` (see section 2.4 of [Imagen
133	            Video](https://huggingface.co/papers/2210.02303) paper).
134	        timestep_spacing (`"linspace"`, `"leading"`, or `"trailing"`, defaults to `"linspace"`):
135	            The way the timesteps should be scaled. Refer to Table 2 of the [Common Diffusion Noise Schedules and
136	            Sample Steps are Flawed](https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891) for more information.
137	        steps_offset (`int`, defaults to `0`):
138	            An offset added to the inference steps, as required by some model families.
139	    """
140	
141	    _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
142	    order = 1
143	
144	    @register_to_config
145	    def __init__(
146	        self,
147	        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
148	        beta_start: float = 0.0001,
149	        beta_end: float = 0.02,
150	        beta_schedule: str = "linear",
151	        trained_betas: np.ndarray | list[float] | None = None,
152	        use_karras_sigmas: bool = False,
153	        use_exponential_sigmas: bool = False,
154	        use_beta_sigmas: bool = False,
155	        prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"] = "epsilon",
156	        timestep_spacing: Literal["linspace", "leading", "trailing"] = "linspace",
157	        steps_offset: int = 0,
158	    ):
159	        if sum([self.config.use_beta_sigmas, self.config.use_exponential_sigmas, self.config.use_karras_sigmas]) > 1:
160	            raise ValueError(
161	                "Only one of `config.use_beta_sigmas`, `config.use_exponential_sigmas`, `config.use_karras_sigmas` can be used."
162	            )
163	        if trained_betas is not None:
164	            self.betas = torch.tensor(trained_betas, dtype=torch.float32)
165	        elif beta_schedule == "linear":
166	            self.betas = torch.linspace(beta_start, beta_end, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32)
167	        elif beta_schedule == "scaled_linear":
168	            # this schedule is very specific to the latent diffusion model.
169	            self.betas = torch.linspace(beta_start**0.5, beta_end**0.5, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32) ** 2
170	        elif beta_schedule == "squaredcos_cap_v2":
171	            # Glide cosine schedule
172	            self.betas = betas_for_alpha_bar(num_train_timesteps)
173	        else:
174	            raise NotImplementedError(f"{beta_schedule} is not implemented for {self.__class__}")
175	
176	        self.alphas = 1.0 - self.betas
177	        self.alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(self.alphas, dim=0)
178	
179	        sigmas = np.array(((1 - self.alphas_cumprod) / self.alphas_cumprod) ** 0.5)
180	        sigmas = np.concatenate([sigmas[::-1], [0.0]]).astype(np.float32)
181	        self.sigmas = torch.from_numpy(sigmas)
182	
183	        # setable values
184	        self.num_inference_steps = None
185	        self.use_karras_sigmas = use_karras_sigmas
186	        self.set_timesteps(num_train_timesteps, None)
187	        self.derivatives = []
188	        self.is_scale_input_called = False
189	
190	        self._step_index = None
191	        self._begin_index = None
192	        self.sigmas = self.sigmas.to("cpu")  # to avoid too much CPU/GPU communication
193	
194	    @property
195	    def init_noise_sigma(self) -> float | torch.Tensor:
196	        """
197	        The standard deviation of the initial noise distribution.
198	
199	        Returns:
200	            `float` or `torch.Tensor`:
201	                The standard deviation of the initial noise distribution, computed based on the maximum sigma value and
202	                the timestep spacing configuration.
203	        """
204	        # standard deviation of the initial noise distribution
205	        if self.config.timestep_spacing in ["linspace", "trailing"]:
206	            return self.sigmas.max()
207	
208	        return (self.sigmas.max() ** 2 + 1) ** 0.5
209	
210	    @property
211	    def step_index(self) -> int:
212	        """
213	        The index counter for current timestep. It will increase by 1 after each scheduler step.
214	
215	        Returns:
216	            `int` or `None`:
217	                The current step index, or `None` if not initialized.
218	        """
219	        return self._step_index
220	
221	    @property
222	    def begin_index(self) -> int:
223	        """
224	        The index for the first timestep. It should be set from pipeline with `set_begin_index` method.
225	
226	        Returns:
227	            `int` or `None`:
228	                The begin index for the scheduler, or `None` if not set.
229	        """
230	        return self._begin_index

232	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_dpmsolver_multistep.DPMSolverMultistepScheduler.set_begin_index
233	    def set_begin_index(self, begin_index: int = 0) -> None:
234	        """
235	        Sets the begin index for the scheduler. This function should be run from pipeline before the inference.
236	
237	        Args:
238	            begin_index (`int`, defaults to `0`):
239	                The begin index for the scheduler.
240	        """
241	        self._begin_index = begin_index
242	
243	    def scale_model_input(self, sample: torch.Tensor, timestep: float | torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
244	        """
245	        Ensures interchangeability with schedulers that need to scale the denoising model input depending on the
246	        current timestep.
247	
248	        Args:
249	            sample (`torch.Tensor`):
250	                The input sample.
251	            timestep (`float` or `torch.Tensor`):
252	                The current timestep in the diffusion chain.
253	
254	        Returns:
255	            `torch.Tensor`:
256	                A scaled input sample.
257	        """
258	
259	        if self.step_index is None:
260	            self._init_step_index(timestep)
261	
262	        sigma = self.sigmas[self.step_index]
263	        sample = sample / ((sigma**2 + 1) ** 0.5)
264	        self.is_scale_input_called = True
265	        return sample
266	
267	    def get_lms_coefficient(self, order: int, t: int, current_order: int) -> float:
268	        """
269	        Compute the linear multistep coefficient.
270	
271	        Args:
272	            order (`int`):
273	                The order of the linear multistep method.
274	            t (`int`):
275	                The current timestep index.
276	            current_order (`int`):
277	                The current order for which to compute the coefficient.
278	
279	        Returns:
280	            `float`:
281	                The computed linear multistep coefficient.
282	        """
283	
284	        def lms_derivative(tau):
285	            prod = 1.0
286	            for k in range(order):
287	                if current_order == k:
288	                    continue
289	                prod *= (tau - self.sigmas[t - k]) / (self.sigmas[t - current_order] - self.sigmas[t - k])
290	            return prod
291	
292	        integrated_coeff = integrate.quad(lms_derivative, self.sigmas[t], self.sigmas[t + 1], epsrel=1e-4)[0]
293	
294	        return integrated_coeff
295	
296	    def set_timesteps(self, num_inference_steps: int, device: str | torch.device = None):
297	        """
298	        Sets the discrete timesteps used for the diffusion chain (to be run before inference).
299	
300	        Args:
301	            num_inference_steps (`int`):
302	                The number of diffusion steps used when generating samples with a pre-trained model.
303	            device (`str` or `torch.device`, *optional*):
304	                The device to which the timesteps should be moved to. If `None`, the timesteps are not moved.
305	        """
306	        self.num_inference_steps = num_inference_steps
307	
308	        # "linspace", "leading", "trailing" corresponds to annotation of Table 2. of https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891
309	        if self.config.timestep_spacing == "linspace":
310	            timesteps = np.linspace(0, self.config.num_train_timesteps - 1, num_inference_steps, dtype=np.float32)[
311	                ::-1
312	            ].copy()
313	        elif self.config.timestep_spacing == "leading":
314	            step_ratio = self.config.num_train_timesteps // self.num_inference_steps
315	            # creates integer timesteps by multiplying by ratio
316	            # casting to int to avoid issues when num_inference_step is power of 3
317	            timesteps = (np.arange(0, num_inference_steps) * step_ratio).round()[::-1].copy().astype(np.float32)
318	            timesteps += self.config.steps_offset
319	        elif self.config.timestep_spacing == "trailing":
320	            step_ratio = self.config.num_train_timesteps / self.num_inference_steps
321	            # creates integer timesteps by multiplying by ratio
322	            # casting to int to avoid issues when num_inference_step is power of 3
323	            timesteps = (np.arange(self.config.num_train_timesteps, 0, -step_ratio)).round().copy().astype(np.float32)
324	            timesteps -= 1
325	        else:
326	            raise ValueError(
327	                f"{self.config.timestep_spacing} is not supported. Please make sure to choose one of 'linspace', 'leading' or 'trailing'."
328	            )
329	
330	        sigmas = np.array(((1 - self.alphas_cumprod) / self.alphas_cumprod) ** 0.5)
331	        log_sigmas = np.log(sigmas)
332	        sigmas = np.interp(timesteps, np.arange(0, len(sigmas)), sigmas)
333	
334	        if self.config.use_karras_sigmas:
335	            sigmas = self._convert_to_karras(in_sigmas=sigmas)
336	            timesteps = np.array([self._sigma_to_t(sigma, log_sigmas) for sigma in sigmas])
337	        elif self.config.use_exponential_sigmas:
338	            sigmas = self._convert_to_exponential(in_sigmas=sigmas, num_inference_steps=num_inference_steps)
339	            timesteps = np.array([self._sigma_to_t(sigma, log_sigmas) for sigma in sigmas])
340	        elif self.config.use_beta_sigmas:
341	            sigmas = self._convert_to_beta(in_sigmas=sigmas, num_inference_steps=num_inference_steps)
342	            timesteps = np.array([self._sigma_to_t(sigma, log_sigmas) for sigma in sigmas])
343	
344	        sigmas = np.concatenate([sigmas, [0.0]]).astype(np.float32)
345	
346	        self.sigmas = torch.from_numpy(sigmas).to(device=device)
347	        self.timesteps = torch.from_numpy(timesteps).to(device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
348	        self._step_index = None
349	        self._begin_index = None
350	        self.sigmas = self.sigmas.to("cpu")  # to avoid too much CPU/GPU communication
351	
352	        self.derivatives = []
353	
354	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_euler_discrete.EulerDiscreteScheduler.index_for_timestep
355	    def index_for_timestep(
356	        self, timestep: float | torch.Tensor, schedule_timesteps: torch.Tensor | None = None
357	    ) -> int:
358	        """
359	        Find the index of a given timestep in the timestep schedule.
360	
361	        Args:
362	            timestep (`float` or `torch.Tensor`):
363	                The timestep value to find in the schedule.
364	            schedule_timesteps (`torch.Tensor`, *optional*):
365	                The timestep schedule to search in. If `None`, uses `self.timesteps`.
366	
367	        Returns:
368	            `int`:
369	                The index of the timestep in the schedule. For the very first step, returns the second index if
370	                multiple matches exist to avoid skipping a sigma when starting mid-schedule (e.g., for image-to-image).
371	        """
372	        if schedule_timesteps is None:
373	            schedule_timesteps = self.timesteps
374	
375	        indices = (schedule_timesteps == timestep).nonzero()
376	
377	        # The sigma index that is taken for the **very** first `step`
378	        # is always the second index (or the last index if there is only 1)
379	        # This way we can ensure we don't accidentally skip a sigma in
380	        # case we start in the middle of the denoising schedule (e.g. for image-to-image)
381	        pos = 1 if len(indices) > 1 else 0
382	
383	        return indices[pos].item()
384	
385	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_euler_discrete.EulerDiscreteScheduler._init_step_index
386	    def _init_step_index(self, timestep: float | torch.Tensor) -> None:
387	        """
388	        Initialize the step index for the scheduler based on the given timestep.
389	
390	        Args:
391	            timestep (`float` or `torch.Tensor`):
392	                The current timestep to initialize the step index from.
393	        """
394	        if self.begin_index is None:
395	            if isinstance(timestep, torch.Tensor):
396	                timestep = timestep.to(self.timesteps.device)
397	            self._step_index = self.index_for_timestep(timestep)
398	        else:
399	            self._step_index = self._begin_index
400	
401	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_euler_discrete.EulerDiscreteScheduler._sigma_to_t
402	    def _sigma_to_t(self, sigma: np.ndarray, log_sigmas: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
403	        """
404	        Convert sigma values to corresponding timestep values through interpolation.
405	
406	        Args:
407	            sigma (`np.ndarray`):
408	                The sigma value(s) to convert to timestep(s).
409	            log_sigmas (`np.ndarray`):
410	                The logarithm of the sigma schedule used for interpolation.
411	
412	        Returns:
413	            `np.ndarray`:
414	                The interpolated timestep value(s) corresponding to the input sigma(s).
415	        """
416	        # get log sigma
417	        log_sigma = np.log(np.maximum(sigma, 1e-10))
418	
419	        # get distribution
420	        dists = log_sigma - log_sigmas[:, np.newaxis]
421	
422	        # get sigmas range
423	        low_idx = np.cumsum((dists >= 0), axis=0).argmax(axis=0).clip(max=log_sigmas.shape[0] - 2)
424	        high_idx = low_idx + 1
425	
426	        low = log_sigmas[low_idx]
427	        high = log_sigmas[high_idx]
428	
429	        # interpolate sigmas
430	        w = (low - log_sigma) / (low - high)
431	        w = np.clip(w, 0, 1)
432	
433	        # transform interpolation to time range
434	        t = (1 - w) * low_idx + w * high_idx
435	        t = t.reshape(sigma.shape)
436	        return t
437	
438	    def _convert_to_karras(self, in_sigmas: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
439	        """
440	        Construct the noise schedule as proposed in [Elucidating the Design Space of Diffusion-Based Generative
441	        Models](https://huggingface.co/papers/2206.00364).
442	
443	        Args:
444	            in_sigmas (`torch.Tensor`):
445	                The input sigma values to be converted.
446	
447	        Returns:
448	            `torch.Tensor`:
449	                The converted sigma values following the Karras noise schedule.
450	        """
451	
452	        sigma_min: float = in_sigmas[-1].item()
453	        sigma_max: float = in_sigmas[0].item()
454	
455	        rho = 7.0  # 7.0 is the value used in the paper
456	        ramp = np.linspace(0, 1, self.num_inference_steps)
457	        min_inv_rho = sigma_min ** (1 / rho)
458	        max_inv_rho = sigma_max ** (1 / rho)
459	        sigmas = (max_inv_rho + ramp * (min_inv_rho - max_inv_rho)) ** rho
460	        return sigmas
461	
462	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_euler_discrete.EulerDiscreteScheduler._convert_to_exponential
463	    def _convert_to_exponential(self, in_sigmas: torch.Tensor, num_inference_steps: int) -> torch.Tensor:
464	        """
465	        Construct an exponential noise schedule.
466	
467	        Args:
468	            in_sigmas (`torch.Tensor`):
469	                The input sigma values to be converted.
470	            num_inference_steps (`int`):
471	                The number of inference steps to generate the noise schedule for.
472	
473	        Returns:
474	            `torch.Tensor`:
475	                The converted sigma values following an exponential schedule.
476	        """
477	
478	        # Hack to make sure that other schedulers which copy this function don't break
479	        # TODO: Add this logic to the other schedulers
480	        if hasattr(self.config, "sigma_min"):
481	            sigma_min = self.config.sigma_min
482	        else:
483	            sigma_min = None
484	
485	        if hasattr(self.config, "sigma_max"):
486	            sigma_max = self.config.sigma_max
487	        else:
488	            sigma_max = None
489	
490	        sigma_min = sigma_min if sigma_min is not None else in_sigmas[-1].item()
491	        sigma_max = sigma_max if sigma_max is not None else in_sigmas[0].item()
492	
493	        sigmas = np.exp(np.linspace(math.log(sigma_max), math.log(sigma_min), num_inference_steps))
494	        return sigmas
495	
496	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_euler_discrete.EulerDiscreteScheduler._convert_to_beta
497	    def _convert_to_beta(
498	        self, in_sigmas: torch.Tensor, num_inference_steps: int, alpha: float = 0.6, beta: float = 0.6
499	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
500	        """
501	        Construct a beta noise schedule as proposed in [Beta Sampling is All You
502	        Need](https://huggingface.co/papers/2407.12173).
503	
504	        Args:
505	            in_sigmas (`torch.Tensor`):
506	                The input sigma values to be converted.
507	            num_inference_steps (`int`):
508	                The number of inference steps to generate the noise schedule for.
509	            alpha (`float`, *optional*, defaults to `0.6`):
510	                The alpha parameter for the beta distribution.
511	            beta (`float`, *optional*, defaults to `0.6`):
512	                The beta parameter for the beta distribution.
513	
514	        Returns:
515	            `torch.Tensor`:
516	                The converted sigma values following a beta distribution schedule.
517	        """
518	
519	        # Hack to make sure that other schedulers which copy this function don't break
520	        # TODO: Add this logic to the other schedulers
521	        if hasattr(self.config, "sigma_min"):
522	            sigma_min = self.config.sigma_min
523	        else:
524	            sigma_min = None
525	
526	        if hasattr(self.config, "sigma_max"):
527	            sigma_max = self.config.sigma_max
528	        else:
529	            sigma_max = None
530	
531	        sigma_min = sigma_min if sigma_min is not None else in_sigmas[-1].item()
532	        sigma_max = sigma_max if sigma_max is not None else in_sigmas[0].item()
533	
534	        sigmas = np.array(
535	            [
536	                sigma_min + (ppf * (sigma_max - sigma_min))
537	                for ppf in [
538	                    scipy.stats.beta.ppf(timestep, alpha, beta)
539	                    for timestep in 1 - np.linspace(0, 1, num_inference_steps)
540	                ]
541	            ]
542	        )
543	        return sigmas
544	
545	    def step(
546	        self,
547	        model_output: torch.Tensor,
548	        timestep: float | torch.Tensor,
549	        sample: torch.Tensor,
550	        order: int = 4,
551	        return_dict: bool = True,
552	    ) -> LMSDiscreteSchedulerOutput | tuple:
553	        """
554	        Predict the sample from the previous timestep by reversing the SDE. This function propagates the diffusion
555	        process from the learned model outputs (most often the predicted noise).
556	
557	        Args:
558	            model_output (`torch.Tensor`):
559	                The direct output from learned diffusion model.
560	            timestep (`float` or `torch.Tensor`):
561	                The current discrete timestep in the diffusion chain.
562	            sample (`torch.Tensor`):
563	                A current instance of a sample created by the diffusion process.
564	            order (`int`, defaults to 4):
565	                The order of the linear multistep method.
566	            return_dict (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `True`):
567	                Whether or not to return a [`~schedulers.scheduling_utils.SchedulerOutput`] or tuple.
568	
569	        Returns:
570	            [`~schedulers.scheduling_utils.SchedulerOutput`] or `tuple`:
571	                If return_dict is `True`, [`~schedulers.scheduling_utils.SchedulerOutput`] is returned, otherwise a
572	                tuple is returned where the first element is the sample tensor.
573	
574	        """
575	        if not self.is_scale_input_called:
576	            warnings.warn(
577	                "The `scale_model_input` function should be called before `step` to ensure correct denoising. "
578	                "See `StableDiffusionPipeline` for a usage example."
579	            )
580	
581	        if self.step_index is None:
582	            self._init_step_index(timestep)
583	
584	        sigma = self.sigmas[self.step_index]
585	
586	        # 1. compute predicted original sample (x_0) from sigma-scaled predicted noise
587	        if self.config.prediction_type == "epsilon":
588	            pred_original_sample = sample - sigma * model_output
589	        elif self.config.prediction_type == "v_prediction":
590	            # * c_out + input * c_skip
591	            pred_original_sample = model_output * (-sigma / (sigma**2 + 1) ** 0.5) + (sample / (sigma**2 + 1))
592	        elif self.config.prediction_type == "sample":
593	            pred_original_sample = model_output
594	        else:
595	            raise ValueError(
596	                f"prediction_type given as {self.config.prediction_type} must be one of `epsilon`, or `v_prediction`"
597	            )
598	
599	        # 2. Convert to an ODE derivative
600	        derivative = (sample - pred_original_sample) / sigma
601	        self.derivatives.append(derivative)
602	        if len(self.derivatives) > order:
603	            self.derivatives.pop(0)
604	
605	        # 3. Compute linear multistep coefficients
606	        order = min(self.step_index + 1, order)
607	        lms_coeffs = [self.get_lms_coefficient(order, self.step_index, curr_order) for curr_order in range(order)]
608	
609	        # 4. Compute previous sample based on the derivatives path
610	        prev_sample = sample + sum(
611	            coeff * derivative for coeff, derivative in zip(lms_coeffs, reversed(self.derivatives))
612	        )
613	
614	        # upon completion increase step index by one
615	        self._step_index += 1
616	
617	        if not return_dict:
618	            return (
619	                prev_sample,
620	                pred_original_sample,
621	            )
622	
623	        return LMSDiscreteSchedulerOutput(prev_sample=prev_sample, pred_original_sample=pred_original_sample)
624	
625	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_euler_discrete.EulerDiscreteScheduler.add_noise
626	    def add_noise(
627	        self,
628	        original_samples: torch.Tensor,
629	        noise: torch.Tensor,
630	        timesteps: torch.Tensor,
631	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
632	        """
633	        Add noise to the original samples according to the noise schedule at the specified timesteps.
634	
635	        Args:
636	            original_samples (`torch.Tensor`):
637	                The original samples to which noise will be added.
638	            noise (`torch.Tensor`):
639	                The noise tensor to add to the original samples.
640	            timesteps (`torch.Tensor`):
641	                The timesteps at which to add noise, determining the noise level from the schedule.
642	
643	        Returns:
644	            `torch.Tensor`:
645	                The noisy samples with added noise scaled according to the timestep schedule.
646	        """
647	        # Make sure sigmas and timesteps have the same device and dtype as original_samples
648	        sigmas = self.sigmas.to(device=original_samples.device, dtype=original_samples.dtype)
649	        if original_samples.device.type == "mps" and torch.is_floating_point(timesteps):
650	            # mps does not support float64
651	            schedule_timesteps = self.timesteps.to(original_samples.device, dtype=torch.float32)
652	            timesteps = timesteps.to(original_samples.device, dtype=torch.float32)
653	        else:
654	            schedule_timesteps = self.timesteps.to(original_samples.device)
655	            timesteps = timesteps.to(original_samples.device)
656	
657	        # self.begin_index is None when scheduler is used for training, or pipeline does not implement set_begin_index
658	        if self.begin_index is None:
659	            step_indices = [self.index_for_timestep(t, schedule_timesteps) for t in timesteps]
660	        elif self.step_index is not None:
661	            # add_noise is called after first denoising step (for inpainting)
662	            step_indices = [self.step_index] * timesteps.shape[0]
663	        else:
664	            # add noise is called before first denoising step to create initial latent(img2img)
665	            step_indices = [self.begin_index] * timesteps.shape[0]
666	
667	        sigma = sigmas[step_indices].flatten()
668	        while len(sigma.shape) < len(original_samples.shape):
669	            sigma = sigma.unsqueeze(-1)
670	
671	        noisy_samples = original_samples + noise * sigma
672	        return noisy_samples
673	
674	    def __len__(self) -> int:
675	        return self.config.num_train_timesteps

74	class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
75	    """
76	    Base class for all schedulers.
77	
78	    [`SchedulerMixin`] contains common functions shared by all schedulers such as general loading and saving
79	    functionalities.
80	
81	    [`ConfigMixin`] takes care of storing the configuration attributes (like `num_train_timesteps`) that are passed to
82	    the scheduler's `__init__` function, and the attributes can be accessed by `scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps`.
83	
84	    Class attributes:
85	        - **_compatibles** (`list[str]`) -- A list of scheduler classes that are compatible with the parent scheduler
86	          class. Use [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] to load a different compatible scheduler class (should be overridden
87	          by parent class).
88	    """
89	
90	    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME
91	    _compatibles = []
92	    has_compatibles = True
93	
94	    @classmethod
95	    @validate_hf_hub_args
96	    def from_pretrained(
97	        cls,
98	        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike | None = None,
99	        subfolder: str | None = None,
100	        return_unused_kwargs=False,
101	        **kwargs,
102	    ) -> Self:
103	        r"""
104	        Instantiate a scheduler from a pre-defined JSON configuration file in a local directory or Hub repository.
105	
106	        Parameters:
107	            pretrained_model_name_or_path (`str` or `os.PathLike`, *optional*):
108	                Can be either:
109	
110	                    - A string, the *model id* (for example `google/ddpm-celebahq-256`) of a pretrained model hosted on
111	                      the Hub.
112	                    - A path to a *directory* (for example `./my_model_directory`) containing the scheduler
113	                      configuration saved with [`~SchedulerMixin.save_pretrained`].
114	            subfolder (`str`, *optional*):
115	                The subfolder location of a model file within a larger model repository on the Hub or locally.
116	            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
117	                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
118	            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):
119	                Path to a directory where a downloaded pretrained model configuration is cached if the standard cache
120	                is not used.
121	            force_download (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
122	                Whether or not to force the (re-)download of the model weights and configuration files, overriding the
123	                cached versions if they exist.
124	
125	            proxies (`dict[str, str]`, *optional*):
126	                A dictionary of proxy servers to use by protocol or endpoint, for example, `{'http': 'foo.bar:3128',
127	                'http://hostname': 'foo.bar:4012'}`. The proxies are used on each request.
128	            output_loading_info(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
129	                Whether or not to also return a dictionary containing missing keys, unexpected keys and error messages.
130	            local_files_only(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
131	                Whether to only load local model weights and configuration files or not. If set to `True`, the model
132	                won't be downloaded from the Hub.
133	            token (`str` or *bool*, *optional*):
134	                The token to use as HTTP bearer authorization for remote files. If `True`, the token generated from
135	                `diffusers-cli login` (stored in `~/.huggingface`) is used.
136	            revision (`str`, *optional*, defaults to `"main"`):
137	                The specific model version to use. It can be a branch name, a tag name, a commit id, or any identifier
138	                allowed by Git.
139	
140	        > [!TIP] > To use private or [gated models](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/models-gated#gated-models), log-in
141	        with `hf > auth login`. You can also activate the special >
142	        ["offline-mode"](https://huggingface.co/diffusers/installation.html#offline-mode) to use this method in a >
143	        firewalled environment.
144	
145	        """
146	        config, kwargs, commit_hash = cls.load_config(
147	            pretrained_model_name_or_path=pretrained_model_name_or_path,
148	            subfolder=subfolder,
149	            return_unused_kwargs=True,
150	            return_commit_hash=True,
151	            **kwargs,
152	        )
153	        return cls.from_config(config, return_unused_kwargs=return_unused_kwargs, **kwargs)
154	
155	    def save_pretrained(self, save_directory: str | os.PathLike, push_to_hub: bool = False, **kwargs):
156	        """
157	        Save a scheduler configuration object to a directory so that it can be reloaded using the
158	        [`~SchedulerMixin.from_pretrained`] class method.
159	
160	        Args:
161	            save_directory (`str` or `os.PathLike`):
162	                Directory where the configuration JSON file will be saved (will be created if it does not exist).
163	            push_to_hub (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
164	                Whether or not to push your model to the Hugging Face Hub after saving it. You can specify the
165	                repository you want to push to with `repo_id` (will default to the name of `save_directory` in your
166	                namespace).
167	            kwargs (`dict[str, Any]`, *optional*):
168	                Additional keyword arguments passed along to the [`~utils.PushToHubMixin.push_to_hub`] method.
169	        """
170	        self.save_config(save_directory=save_directory, push_to_hub=push_to_hub, **kwargs)
171	
172	    @property
173	    def compatibles(self):
174	        """
175	        Returns all schedulers that are compatible with this scheduler
176	
177	        Returns:
178	            `list[SchedulerMixin]`: list of compatible schedulers
179	        """
180	        return self._get_compatibles()
181	
182	    @classmethod
183	    def _get_compatibles(cls):
184	        compatible_classes_str = list(set([cls.__name__] + cls._compatibles))
185	        diffusers_library = importlib.import_module(__name__.split(".")[0])
186	        compatible_classes = [
187	            getattr(diffusers_library, c) for c in compatible_classes_str if hasattr(diffusers_library, c)
188	        ]
189	        return compatible_classes

27	def is_tensor(x) -> bool:
28	    """
29	    Tests if `x` is a `torch.Tensor` or `np.ndarray`.
30	    """
31	    if is_torch_available():
32	        import torch
33	
34	        if isinstance(x, torch.Tensor):
35	            return True
36	
37	    return isinstance(x, np.ndarray)

40	class BaseOutput(OrderedDict):
41	    """
42	    Base class for all model outputs as dataclass. Has a `__getitem__` that allows indexing by integer or slice (like a
43	    tuple) or strings (like a dictionary) that will ignore the `None` attributes. Otherwise behaves like a regular
44	    Python dictionary.
45	
46	    > [!WARNING] > You can't unpack a [`BaseOutput`] directly. Use the [`~utils.BaseOutput.to_tuple`] method to convert
47	    it to a tuple > first.
48	    """
49	
50	    def __init_subclass__(cls) -> None:
51	        """Register subclasses as pytree nodes.

- Otherwise: generates timesteps using linspace/leading/trailing strategies

```python
def step(

) -> EulerDiscreteSchedulerOutput | tuple:
```
**Return:** `EulerDiscreteSchedulerOutput` with `prev_sample` and `pred_original_sample`, or tuple of same

3. Initializes step index
4. Upcasts to float32

6. Adds noise if gamma > 0 (Karras stochasticity)
7. Predicts original sample based on prediction_type (epsilon, sample, original_sample, v_prediction)
8. Computes ODE derivative: `(sample - pred_original_sample) / sigma_hat`
9. Computes dt (sigma change)
10. Updates sample: `sample + derivative * dt`
11. Increments step index
12. Returns output


- Handles MPS dtype limitations
- Gets step indices from timesteps

- Gets sigma values for step indices
- Broadcasts dimensions
- Computes: `noisy_samples = original_samples + noise * sigma`

- PyTorch-based schedulers wrapped in `if is_torch_available()` block
- Flax schedulers wrapped in `if is_flax_available()` block

- TorchSDE-dependent schedulers wrapped in `if is_torch_available() and is_torchsde_available()` block

**Registration Method:**
1. **Build Phase**: Creates `_import_structure` dict mapping module names to class lists
2. **Lazy Module Pattern**: Uses `_LazyModule` for deferred imports when `TYPE_CHECKING or DIFFUSERS_SLOW_IMPORT` is False
3. **TYPE_CHECKING/Slow Import Block**: Direct imports when type checking or slow imports enabled
4. **Dummy Objects**: Provides dummy classes when optional dependencies unavailable

**DDPMScheduler Registration:**


**EulerDiscreteScheduler Registration:**
```python

```


```python

```

#### Total Registered Schedulers
The __init__.py registers approximately 30+ different scheduler implementations across PyTorch, Flax, and special dependency variants.

| Pattern | DDPM | Euler |
|---------|------|-------|
| **Parent Classes** | `SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin` | `SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin` |
| **@register_to_config** | Yes, on `__init__` | Yes, on `__init__` |
| **Output Dataclass** | `DDPMSchedulerOutput` | `EulerDiscreteSchedulerOutput` |
| **Beta Schedules** | 5 types (linear, scaled_linear, squaredcos_cap_v2, laplace, sigmoid) | 3 types (linear, scaled_linear, squaredcos_cap_v2) |
| **Sigma Computation** | Via alphas_cumprod | Via alphas_cumprod with special handling |
| **Timestep Spacing** | linspace, leading, trailing | linspace, leading, trailing |
| **add_noise Formula** | `sqrt_alpha * original + sqrt(1-alpha) * noise` | `original + sigma * noise` |
| **step() Complexity** | Variance computation, prediction type conversion | Stochasticity (s_churn), ODE integration |
| **Helper Methods** | _get_variance, _threshold_sample, get_velocity | _sigma_to_t, _convert_to_karras/exponential/beta, index_for_timestep |

## 3. CONFIGURATION BASE CLASS: `src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py`

### ConfigMixin Class (lines 88-176)
This is the base class that all schedulers inherit from. It handles configuration storage and retrieval.

```python
class ConfigMixin:
    r"""
    Base class for all configuration classes. All configuration parameters are stored under `self.config`. 
    Also provides the [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] and [`~ConfigMixin.save_config`] methods for loading, 
    downloading, and saving classes that inherit from [`ConfigMixin`].
    
    Class attributes:
        - **config_name** (`str`) -- A filename under which the config should stored
        - **ignore_for_config** (`list[str]`) -- A list of attributes that should not be saved
        - **has_compatibles** (`bool`) -- Whether the class has compatible classes
        - **_deprecated_kwargs** (`list[str]`) -- Keyword arguments that are deprecated
    """

    config_name = None
    ignore_for_config = []
    has_compatibles = False
    _deprecated_kwargs = []
    _auto_class = None

    def register_to_config(self, **kwargs):
        """Registers config parameters to self._internal_dict as FrozenDict"""
        if self.config_name is None:
            raise NotImplementedError(f"Make sure that {self.__class__} has defined a class name `config_name`")
        
        kwargs.pop("kwargs", None)  # Special case for deprecation warnings
        
        if not hasattr(self, "_internal_dict"):
            internal_dict = kwargs
        else:
            previous_dict = dict(self._internal_dict)
            internal_dict = {**self._internal_dict, **kwargs}
        
        self._internal_dict = FrozenDict(internal_dict)

    @property
    def config(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Returns the config of the class as a frozen dictionary"""
        return self._internal_dict
```

### @register_to_config Decorator (lines 690-738)
This decorator automatically registers all `__init__` parameters to the config:

```python
def register_to_config(init):
    r"""
    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from [`ConfigMixin`] so that all the arguments are
    automatically sent to `self.register_for_config`. To ignore a specific argument accepted by the init but that
    shouldn't be registered in the config, use the `ignore_for_config` class variable

    Warning: Once decorated, all private arguments (beginning with an underscore) are trashed and not sent to the init!
    """

    @functools.wraps(init)
    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
        # Ignore private kwargs in the init.
        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
        
        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
            )

        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
        new_kwargs = {}
        signature = inspect.signature(init)
        parameters = {
            name: p.default for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) 
            if i > 0 and name not in ignore
        }
        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
            new_kwargs[name] = arg

        # Then add all kwargs
        new_kwargs.update({
            k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
            for k, default in parameters.items()
            if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
        })

        # Take note of parameters not present in loaded config
        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))

        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)

    return inner_init
```

### SchedulerMixin Class (lines 74-189)

```python
class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
    """
    Base class for all schedulers.

    [`SchedulerMixin`] contains common functions shared by all schedulers such as general loading and saving
    functionalities.

    [`ConfigMixin`] takes care of storing the configuration attributes (like `num_train_timesteps`) that are passed to
    the scheduler's `__init__` function, and the attributes can be accessed by `scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps`.

    Class attributes:
        - **_compatibles** (`list[str]`) -- A list of scheduler classes that are compatible with the parent scheduler
          class. Use [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] to load a different compatible scheduler class.
    """

    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME  # "scheduler_config.json"
    _compatibles = []
    has_compatibles = True

    @classmethod
    def from_pretrained(
        cls,
        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike | None = None,
        subfolder: str | None = None,
        return_unused_kwargs=False,
        **kwargs,
    ) -> Self:
        """Instantiate a scheduler from a pre-defined JSON configuration file"""
        config, kwargs, commit_hash = cls.load_config(
            pretrained_model_name_or_path=pretrained_model_name_or_path,
            subfolder=subfolder,
            return_unused_kwargs=True,
            return_commit_hash=True,
            **kwargs,
        )
        return cls.from_config(config, return_unused_kwargs=return_unused_kwargs, **kwargs)

    @classmethod
    def _get_compatibles(cls):
        compatible_classes_str = list(set([cls.__name__] + cls._compatibles))
        diffusers_library = importlib.import_module(__name__.split(".")[0])
        compatible_classes = [
            getattr(diffusers_library, c) for c in compatible_classes_str if hasattr(diffusers_library, c)
        ]
        return compatible_classes
```

## 3. CONFIGURATION BASE CLASS: `src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py`

### ConfigMixin Class (lines 88-176)
This is the base class that all schedulers inherit from. It handles configuration storage and retrieval.

```python
class ConfigMixin:
    r"""
    Base class for all configuration classes. All configuration parameters are stored under `self.config`. 
    Also provides the [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] and [`~ConfigMixin.save_config`] methods for loading, 
    downloading, and saving classes that inherit from [`ConfigMixin`].
    
    Class attributes:
        - **config_name** (`str`) -- A filename under which the config should stored
        - **ignore_for_config** (`list[str]`) -- A list of attributes that should not be saved
        - **has_compatibles** (`bool`) -- Whether the class has compatible classes
        - **_deprecated_kwargs** (`list[str]`) -- Keyword arguments that are deprecated
    """

    config_name = None
    ignore_for_config = []
    has_compatibles = False
    _deprecated_kwargs = []
    _auto_class = None

    def register_to_config(self, **kwargs):
        """Registers config parameters to self._internal_dict as FrozenDict"""
        if self.config_name is None:
            raise NotImplementedError(f"Make sure that {self.__class__} has defined a class name `config_name`")
        
        kwargs.pop("kwargs", None)  # Special case for deprecation warnings
        
        if not hasattr(self, "_internal_dict"):
            internal_dict = kwargs
        else:
            previous_dict = dict(self._internal_dict)
            internal_dict = {**self._internal_dict, **kwargs}
        
        self._internal_dict = FrozenDict(internal_dict)

    @property
    def config(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Returns the config of the class as a frozen dictionary"""
        return self._internal_dict
```

### @register_to_config Decorator (lines 690-738)
This decorator automatically registers all `__init__` parameters to the config:

```python
def register_to_config(init):
    r"""
    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from [`ConfigMixin`] so that all the arguments are
    automatically sent to `self.register_for_config`. To ignore a specific argument accepted by the init but that
    shouldn't be registered in the config, use the `ignore_for_config` class variable

    Warning: Once decorated, all private arguments (beginning with an underscore) are trashed and not sent to the init!
    """

    @functools.wraps(init)
    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
        # Ignore private kwargs in the init.
        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
        
        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
            )

        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
        new_kwargs = {}
        signature = inspect.signature(init)
        parameters = {
            name: p.default for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) 
            if i > 0 and name not in ignore
        }
        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
            new_kwargs[name] = arg

        # Then add all kwargs
        new_kwargs.update({
            k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
            for k, default in parameters.items()
            if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
        })

        # Take note of parameters not present in loaded config
        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))

        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)

    return inner_init
```

### SchedulerMixin Class (lines 74-189)
